hypothesis: Including edge attributes encoding pairwise thermochemical descriptors (differences in ionization energy, electron affinity, and estimated gas-phase reaction enthalpy) in the GraphSAGE model will increase R2 by at least 0.05 on out-of-distribution ternary mixture etch-rate predictions (e.g., Ar/O2/CF4) relative to an otherwise identical complete-graph model without edge attributes under equal training data and capacity.
